value.body:
## Summary

Evaluate upgrading root `@types/node` from 25.9.2 to 26.0.0.

## Affected manifest

- `package.json`

## Rationale

`pnpm outdated --recursive --format json` reports `@types/node` 26.0.0 as the latest major version. This should not be a blind bump unless the repo's Node/Electron runtime target is intended to track Node 26 types, because Node type majors can change compile-time API assumptions across all packages.

## Recommended approach

- Confirm the supported Node runtime version for CLI/server packages.
- Confirm the Electron-embedded Node version expectations for desktop main/preload code.
- Upgrade if Node 26 types match the intended target; otherwise keep 25.x and document the target.

## Verification

- Run `pnpm typecheck`.
- Run `pnpm --filter @cycle/desktop typecheck`.
- Check for any Node global, filesystem, stream, worker, and process typing changes.
value.title: Evaluate @types/node 26 upgrade
